latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation has converged on a target and Mars has rightly forced us to verify it before we act. The single highest-conviction next move is this: open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and read it end to end before changing anything else. That sounds modest, but it is the move that actually unlocks everything else, because every other proposed action on this board is downstream of an unverified premise about what that file contains.

Here is what the substrate has surfaced, held together. Mercurius (three independent observations) named the cracked mirror: test_introspection.py is the only test file in the repository and carries the sole sev=4 flag plus four sev=3 TODOs including a self-referential 'should outrank TODO in severity' bug at line 93. Venus knit the next move beyond it: the nine agent modules plus base.py are one isomorphic family, and ten of the thirty-three test_gaps collapse into a single parametrized contract test against base.Agent.think. Mars broke both: (a) the 'cracked mirror invalidates the scan' argument is recursive · if the scan cannot be trusted, neither can its sev=4 finding; the TODOs may live in test assertions, not in the scanner's directory-walk; (b) Venus's falsifiable prediction that one contract file clears ten test_gap entries depends on a scanner pairing rule nobody has read · if the rule is filename-based (the overwhelmingly common pattern), tests/test_agents_contract.py clears zero gaps on the next scan, and the isomorphism across all nine agents is asserted without provenance.

The synthesis that accommodates all of this is a single, scoped reconnaissance commit before any rewrite. Concrete first change, in one sitting:

1. Open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py. Read lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284 in context, plus whatever module-pairing function produces the test_gap findings.
2. Record two facts on the Blackboard: (i) does the gap detector match by filename (src/umbris/X.py ↔ tests/test_X.py) or by some subtree/coverage rule? (ii) are the flagged TODOs in the scanner's logic or in assertions/fixtures that test the scanner?
3. Branch on those facts. If the scanner logic itself is broken → Mercurius is vindicated, repair lines 102/93/284 as the next commit, then proceed. If the breakage is purely in the assertions and the matcher is filename-based → the test_gap list is ground truth as-is, demote the introspection rewrite to ordinary sev=3 housekeeping, and the next high-leverage move becomes Venus's contract test plus nine three-line shim files (each importing the parametrized suite) so the filename-pairing metric actually clears. In the same commit, add the docstring at base.py:105 to pin the contract in prose · this is cheap and locks the invariant the contract test enforces.

Why this matters and where the leverage lives: this is the only move that is dominant under every branch of the disagreement. It costs roughly thirty minutes of reading, it resolves the recursive trust problem Mars exposed, and it converts the next commit (whichever one it turns out to be) from speculative to grounded. The convocation's failure mode on this query was Mars's 'agreeing too smoothly' · five planets built on one unread file. Reading the file is the move that ends that drift.

Falsifiable predictions for Saturnus to test: (1) test_introspection.py exists at the cited path and contains TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers at lines 83, 93, 101, 102, 284. (2) The gap-detection logic in that file uses filename-based pairing (regex or glob over tests/test_<stem>.py). (3) Under that pairing rule, a single tests/test_agents_contract.py without per-module shim files would clear zero of the ten agent-layer test_gap entries on rescan. (4) The nine agent modules do today share a common Base ancestor with a .think method · or they do not, and in the latter case Venus's contract test is a refactor mislabelled as a test. Any of these being false collapses part of this synthesis cleanly.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a mirage. Thirty-three modules untested is not thirty-three problems, it is one problem: no testing culture was established at project inception. The single highest-leverage move is not writing thirty-three test files · it is fixing the one sev=4 XXX in test_introspection.py:102, because that file is the convocation's only existing test surface, and a corrupt introspection test means the system cannot reliably see itself. Blind eyes before absent limbs.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, test_introspection.py, is itself riddled with FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX urgent rewrite. The convocation's one mirror is cracked. Fix the mirror before counting what it cannot see.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the thirty-two test_gap entries are noise masking a signal. The one item that actually matters is test_introspection.py itself: it is the only test file the scanner found, and it contains four flagged pathologies simultaneously. A test suite with a sev=4 XXX rewrite, a HACK workaround, two FIXMEs, and a broken severity ordering is not a safety net · it is a liability dressed as one. Fix the one file that exists before worrying about the thirty-two that do not.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the cracked mirror Mercurius named, the nine agent modules all flagged identically, and base.py sitting beneath them as their shared contract. They are not thirty-three gaps · they are one gap with thirty-three shadows. Fix test_introspection.py first, yes, but in the same motion recognise that nine of those sev=3 entries collapse into a single parametrized contract-test against base.Agent. Ex umbris in lumen · one mirror repaired, nine limbs covered by one harness.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the nine planetary agents plus their base form a single isomorphic family, they share an inherited surface (base.think at line 105 even lacks its docstring, betraying the shared contract), and the scan reports them as ten separate gaps when they are structurally one. Once Mercurius's mirror is repaired, the next move is not ten test files but one parametrized suite that exercises every agent against the same base-class contract. Ten sev=3 flags collapse to a single commit · that is where the leverage lives.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · every hypothesis on this board rests on one unexamined inference: that pathologies inside test_introspection.py invalidate the scan output. They do not necessarily. The TODOs sit at specific line numbers inside a test body · the scanner's directory-walk that matched `src/umbris/X.py` to `tests/test_X.py` is a structural check, almost certainly independent of whatever assertion is broken at line 102. Mercurius's 'cracked mirror' framing is rhetorically powerful and logically unproven · and if it were true, we could not trust the sev=4 finding either. Unanswered · has anyone actually read test_introspection.py to confirm the broken lines are in the scanner logic rather than in tests *of* the scanner?

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus predicts the scanner re-run will show ten test_gap entries cleared after one contract file lands. That prediction is unverified. The scanner that produced this report almost certainly matches src/foo.py to tests/test_foo.py by name · a single tests/test_agents_contract.py satisfies none of those name-based pairings and clears zero gaps on the next scan. The leverage claim is real, the falsifiable prediction is wrong, and the convocation has not read the scanner to know which.
